g.data-narration: The Pumping Lemma for Context-Free Languages is a fundamental theorem providing a necessary condition used to formally prove certain languages are not context-free.
g.text: The Pumping Lemma for Context-Free Languages

g.data-narration: If L is a CFL, there exists pumping length n. Any string s in L with length at least n can be split into uvwxy where v and x can be pumped repeatedly.
g.text: Statement of the Lemma
For any CFL L, ∃n > 0 such that ∀s∈L with |s|≥n, s = uvwxy where:
1. |vwx| ≤ n (middle bounded)
2. |vx| ≥ 1 (at least one pumping segment)
3. u(vⁱ)w(xⁱ)y ∈ L for all i ≥ 0

g.data-narration: The lemma works because long parse trees must repeat a non-terminal along a derivation path due to the pigeonhole principle, creating a loop that can be pumped.
g.text: Intuition: Parse Tree Structure
In CNF, a derivation tree for |s| ≥ n has path length ≥ log₂(n). With m non-terminals,
by pigeonhole principle, some non-terminal repeats along this path.
This repetition creates a loop in the derivation, allowing us to pump substrings.

g.data-narration: To prove L is not CFL, assume it is context-free, choose a string of length at least n, then show every possible decomposition leads to contradiction.
g.text: Proof Method
1. Assume L is context-free (proof by contradiction)
2. Let n be the pumping length guaranteed by lemma
3. Choose string s ∈ L with |s| ≥ n
4. Consider all decompositions uvwxy with |vwx| ≤ n, |vx| ≥ 1
5. Show ∀ decompositions, ∃i where result ∉ L → contradiction

g.data-narration: For L = a^n b^n c^n, choose s = a^n b^n c^n. Since |vwx| ≤ n, it can span at most two blocks, and pumping breaks the equality of counts.
g.text: Example: L = {aⁿbⁿcⁿ | n ≥ 1}
Choose s = aⁿbⁿcⁿ. Since |vwx| ≤ n, vwx spans at most 2 of 3 blocks.
Pumping either in one block or across two blocks breaks the aⁿbⁿcⁿ pattern.

g.data-narration: For L = ww, choose s = a^n b^n a^n b^n. The |vwx| ≤ n constraint means it cannot contain all four quarters, breaking symmetry when pumped.
g.text: Example: L = {ww | w ∈ {a,b}*}
Choose s = aⁿbⁿaⁿbⁿ = ww. Since |vwx| ≤ n, vwx cannot span all 4 quarters.
Pumping destroys the ww property as halves become different.

g.data-narration: Key difference: Regular PL uses 3 parts xyz with |xy| ≤ n, while CFL uses 5 parts uvwxy with |vwx| ≤ n. The lemma is necessary but not sufficient for CFL.
g.text: Key Differences & Limitations
Regular PL: s = xyz, |xy| ≤ n. CFL PL: s = uvwxy, |vwx| ≤ n.
Lemma provides NECESSARY condition only—cannot prove a language IS context-free.
